IT IS NOT CLEAR ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS RUN. DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, COUNT ONE, FOR SLANDER OF TITLE IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA DOES NOT HAVE STANDING. DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, COUNT TWO, FOR SLANDER OF TITLE, IS OVERRULED. THE FAC ALLEGES PECUNIARY LOSS. (FAC, 84, 86.) THERE ARE NO FACTS IN THE FAC SUPPORTING THE CLAIM THAT THE TRANSACTION WAS INTENDED TO AFFECT THE PLAINTIFF. (SEELEY V. SEYMOUR (1987) 190 CAL.APP.3D 844, 861.) PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. (EVID. CODE, SECTION 452(D).) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. TAUALA ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT THERESA MURPHY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

is OVERRULED. The untimeliness of cause of action for judicial foreclosure does not clearly and affirmatively appear on the face of the FAC. (Austin v. Medicis (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 577, 585.) Demurrer is not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts and holding an evidentiary hearing. (Joslin v. H.A.S. Ins. Brokerage (1986) 184 Cal.App.3d 369, 374.)
The cause of action for slander of title is not barred by the statute of limitations. (COVID-19 Emergency Rule, 9(a).) 10 days to answer.
Defendant Murphy's request for judicial notice is GRANTED as to the complaint, answer, request to dismiss Defendant Murphy, first amended complaint, and amended motion for leave to file a first amended complaint. Request for judicial notice is denied as to the notice of default and notice of sale for lack of a declaration attesting to the veracity of the documents.
Plaintiff's request for judicial notice GRANTED. (Evid. Code, Section 452(d).)

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED in part, and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer for the first cause of action for judicial foreclosure is overruled. It is not clear on the face of the complaint that the statute of limitations has run.
Demurrer to the second cause of action, count one, for slander of title is overruled. Defendant Bank of America does not have standing.
Demurrer to the second cause of action, count two, for slander of title, is overruled. The FAC alleges pecuniary loss. (FAC, 84, 86.)
Demurrer to the third cause of action for cancellation of instruments is overruled. The FAC alleges the required elements. (U.S. Bank National Assn. v. Naifeh (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 767, 778.)
Demurrer to fourth cause of action for negligence is sustained, with leave to amend, for failure to plead the element of duty. There are no facts in the FAC supporting the claim that the transaction was intended to affect the plaintiff. (Seeley v. Seymour (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 844, 861.)
Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is GRANTED. (Evid. Code, Section 452(d).)

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)
